name: Magnesium L-Threonate
historicalUse: Magnesium L-threonate is a novel chelated form of magnesium developed at MIT in 2010. Its unique blood-brain barrier penetration distinguishes it from all other magnesium forms. The classical tradition of using mineral-rich waters and magnesium-containing foods for cognitive and nervous system support aligns with the modern understanding of magnesium's essential role in synaptic plasticity.
classicalSources: Modern formulation — functional parallels in classical mineral therapeutics
modernEvidence: Magtein® (magnesium L-threonate) was specifically designed to cross the blood-brain barrier, uniquely elevating brain magnesium levels. Dr. Liu Guosong's MIT research demonstrated that Magtein® increases synaptic density, NMDA receptor function, and cognitive flexibility in animal models. Human clinical trials show improvements in cognitive function scores and working memory in middle-aged and older adults.
mechanisms: Blood-brain barrier penetration — threonate chelation enables active transport across BBB
NMDA receptor modulation — magnesium gates NMDA receptors, controlling synaptic plasticity
Synaptic density increase — elevates NR2B NMDA receptor expression
Long-term potentiation enhancement — supports memory consolidation mechanisms
Sleep quality — activates parasympathetic nervous system, supports deep sleep stages
benefits: Supports working memory and cognitive flexibility
Promotes healthy synaptic density and neuroplasticity
Contributes to stress resilience via parasympathetic activation
safetyProfile: Well tolerated. Minimal laxative effect compared to other magnesium forms. Mild drowsiness is a feature, not an adverse effect — take in the evening.
contraindications: Renal impairment — reduced magnesium clearance; use with caution
drugInteractions: Same interactions as other magnesium forms at high doses
physicianNotes: Magtein® is the only magnesium form with clinical evidence for brain-specific cognitive benefit. Standard magnesium forms do not reliably increase brain magnesium. Appropriate in cognitive aging protocols, sleep optimization, and stress resilience programs. Take in the evening for synergistic sleep benefit.
patientGuidance: Best taken in the evening — it supports deep sleep and relaxation. Unlike standard magnesium supplements, this form specifically supports brain health and memory.
